title: hieracium cottetii subsp. erucophyllum
intro: Hieracium cottetii subsp. erucophyllum, also known as hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and milky sap. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Hieracium cottetii subsp. erucophyllum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and act as an antibacterial agent, making it a useful remedy for skin conditions and infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
